adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (ARG -185 / draw +310 / AUT +550 -> de-vig ~62/23/15), which firmed toward Argentina from the pre-fill. Top spot in Group J is on the line -- both won their openers by multiple goals (Argentina 3-0 over Algeria with a Messi hat-trick; Austria 3-1 over Jordan). Argentina are the No.1 side and clear favourites, but Austria are defensively miserly (only six goals conceded in 12 matches), so this is no rout. A 2-0 Argentina win is the central case; the model's 93% is far too high against an organised Austria.

factors.note: Model 93% vs market 62%; anchored to market
No.1 ranked; Messi in form (hat-trick vs Algeria, one off the all-time WC goals record)
Stout defence -- only 6 goals conceded in last 12; won't be blown away

odds.source: covers/CBS 3-way (ARG -185, draw +310, AUT +550) [refresh 2026-06-22; ARG firmed, top-of-group]

adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (FRA -1200 / draw +900 / IRQ +3300 -> de-vig ~88/9/3). France ran out 3-1 winners over Senegal and can effectively seal a knockout spot with another win; Mbappe is fit and in form (two goals already, 56 internationally). Iraq were beaten 4-1 by Norway, their central defence looked vulnerable to direct play, and Alhamadi is doubtful. A comfortable multi-goal France win, around 3-0, is the central case; the model's 97% is a touch high but the market agrees France are overwhelming.

factors.note: Model 97% vs market 88%; anchored to market
Mbappe fit & scoring; deep, elite squad; chasing qualification
Conceded 4 to Norway; central-defence frailties; Alhamadi doubtful

adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (NOR +135 / draw +240 / SEN +210; Kalshi 43/27/30 -> ~41/28/31) -- this is close to a three-way coin-flip, not the model's 59% Norway. Norway topped the group after thumping Iraq 4-1 (Haaland two goals) and a win seals their passage; Senegal lost 3-1 to France and must respond, and they have the pace to hurt Norway. Both-teams-to-score and Over 2.5 are favoured. A 2-1 Norway win is my lean given Haaland's threat, but Senegal and the draw are both very live.

factors.note: Model 59% vs market 41%; anchored to market (near three-way)
Haaland on two goals, most clinical threat in the group; home at MetLife
Must-win after 3-1 loss to France; pace & quality, defensively vulnerable -> open game

adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (ALG -140 / draw +280 / JOR +500 -> de-vig ~58/26/16). Both lost their openers (Jordan 1-3 to Austria, Algeria 0-3 to Argentina) so this is effectively knockout for survival. Algeria are favourites and their broader form is encouraging (3W-1D in the five before Argentina), while Jordan have conceded at least twice in each of their last six. Both defences are leaky, so Over 2.5 is appealing -- a 1-2 Algeria win is the central case, though Jordan at home can make it a shootout.

factors.note: Market ~58% Algeria; anchored to market (away favourite)
3W-1D in the five before the Argentina loss; that 0-3 looks an anomaly
WC debutants; conceded 2+ in each of last six -> leaky, raises goals/Algeria win
